recent_activity.title: Idle connections not being pruned since 4.1
recent_activity.body: We have been using NpgSQL 4.010 with NpgSQL Entity Framework Core 2.2.4 for some time without any issue. We are not yet ready to upgrade to .Net Core 3 so we upgraded NpgSQL to 4.1.1 while leaving Entity Framework at 2.2. As soon as we went live we found our connection count in production steadily increasing until the point when Postgres became unresponsive. Our average connection count in production stays around 1000-1500 between two Aurora instances. After this version went live our connection

recent_activity.title: NullReferenceException when using LoadAsync in DynamoDB with C#
In case of 403 error, the loader keeps loading
recent_activity.body:
At the console it reports:
Broken interceptor detected: Config object not supplied in rejection: ../.../pull50
There are no interceptors in my angular code.

BTW In #177 you mention it has support for error code >= 400, but that nowhere to be found in latest master code?
